item.title: Proud Janitor Moment: I removed the hard water buildup from this urinal! COME AND SEE HOW GOOD IT LOOKS
item.description:
Before and after!
The before! The fixture is covered with hard water deposites. Freakin' ew!
I used chemical (foamy q & a) and let it sit for 5 minutes. After letting the foamy sit, most of the hard water spots came off just by wiping them with a paper towel. For the harder spots I had to use a razor blade.
The after! Look how pretty!
item.dc:creator: Lyricalwordsmith

item.title: Incredibly Rare 13 In 100 Billion Event Seen At CERN Particle Accelerator
item.description: September 25, 2024 James Felton Senior Staff Writer Edited by Maddy Chapman Scientists at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN) have observed an incredibly rare event, which could possibly hint at new physics beyond the Standard Model. There's nothing particle physicists like more than crashing particles together and seeing what comes out of the mess. Such experiments can help us find evidence of particles and processes predicted by the Standard Model of particle physics – such as the Higgs Boson – or sometimes hint that we are missing something, such as the decay of the B meson. “The Standard Model describes the fundamental forces and building blocks of the universe. It is a highly successful theory, but there are several mysteries of the universe that the Standard Model does not explain, such as the nature of dark matter and the origins of the matter–antimatter imbalance in the universe," Professor Mark Thomson, chair of the Science and Technology Facilities Council and contributor to the NA62 experiment, explained in 2020. “Physicists have been searching for theoretical extensions to the Standard Model. Measurements of ultra-rare processes provide an exciting avenue for exploring these possibilities, with the hope of discovering new physics beyond the Standard Model.” One ultra-rare event being probed by the NA62 experiment is the decay of a charged kaon into a charged pion and a neutrino and anti-neutrino pair. Kaons, also known as K mesons, are strange (and sometimes anti-strange) beasts. Protons and neutrons are usually made up of three quarks. For protons that's two up quarks and one down quark, for neutrons, it's one up quark and two down quarks. Kaons contain two quarks, combinations of up quarks and anti-up quarks, strange and anti-strange quarks. Charged kaons (K+) are made of an up quark and an anti-strange quark. They are of particular interest as their decay is very precisely predicted by the Standard Model. According to these predictions, fewer than 1 in 10 billion charged kaons will decay into a charged pion and a neutrino-antineutrino pair (K+ → π+νṽ). The NA62 experiment attempts to search for this decay by colliding a high-intensity proton beam into a stationary target, which then produces secondary particles that can be detected, measured, and identified (though neutrinos are identified through missing energy). In 2020, the team reported evidence of this rare form of decay being detected by the experiment. Now, after far more collisions, including higher-energy collisions, the team reports a 5-sigma detection, meaning there is a 0.00006 percent chance that the detection is a statistical fluke. "With this measurement, K+ → π+νṽ becomes the rarest decay established at discovery level – the famous 5 sigma," Cristina Lazzeroni, Professor in Particle Physics at the University of Birmingham, said in a statement. "This difficult analysis is the result of excellent teamwork, and I am extremely proud of this new result." While the decay is rare, as predicted by the Standard Model, it is around 50 percent higher than expected, occurring about 13 times in 100 billion. It is unclear what causes this discrepancy between the Standard Model's predictions and the results observed, with possible explanations including new particles or new physics, both of which are pretty exciting. "This is the culmination of a long project started more than a decade ago. Looking for effects in nature that have probabilities to happen of the order of 10-11 is fascinating and challenging," Professor Giuseppe Ruggiero, from the University of Florence, added. "After rigorous and painstaking work, we have got a stunning reward to our effort and delivered a long-awaited result." While the team has announced and presented results at CERN, full papers and further experiments will follow. The team presented their findings at a CERN EP seminar. Source: https://www.iflscience.com/incredibly-rare-13-in-100-billion-event-seen-at-cern-particle-accelerator-76091
item.dc:creator: thedoci

item.title: Traffic : Be Predictable, Not "Nice"
item.description: This is a classic three way intersection with a single stop sign on the side road. Red and green meet at the intersection, both turning onto the side road. What should happen : Green has the right of way. They take the turn immediately without stopping. Red stops and waits for their turn. Safe, predictable, normal. What happens : Green stops at the intersection. Red stops at the intersection. Both wait. Green signals via headlights for red to go first. This is confusing, slow, and introduces extra potential for collisions. Why this is dangerous : Cars behind green are not expecting them to stop. Red could potentially make the assumption that this will happen next time, but then next time green doesn't stop. Both are confused and go at the same time. A third car at the stop sign won't know what going on and if it's safe to move or not. Be predictable, not "nice".
item.dc:creator: Kavrae

item.title: The PowerPenz Doodler Pen!
item.description: There sure are a whole lotta wacky Etch A Sketch variants out there! If you're reading this description and happen to have the red variant of this toy, I'd love to chat about how I can take it off your hands :) Also yes, I intentionally dressed to match this glorious hunk of plastic lol

item.title: Memes vol 361 Boobie
item.description:
Imagine falling off
I have a picture of him playing his saxophone in my wallet!!
His cat Big Belly has a ginormous Glock sack
I threw up at a restaurant :( on my plate- while on a date with him. But a week before that he threw up on a ride at the fair so we were even
And big curly hair and huge thighs and facial piercings
We’re 20 so don’t come after us for this one
This is so Dana core
The aforementioned Big Belly tax
Scruffy tax
Froggy and Nessie tax- OH NO I FORGOT LIZA JANE!!
Pickles tax. Happy National Boyfriend day, my sweet boob!! I love you so much!!! @chriscaprisun

item.title: If you make a nostril sound, you have to hit the green arrow thingy. Friday MEME DUMP (hold on, the weekend is almost here)
item.description:
1. So the story goes that the managament of the company asked the animators to make Chel less sexucal as the first interation was too hot. This is the less hot version:
2. Yall remember the San Andreas dancing missions?
3
4
5. hah
6. RELATE HARD
7. Fuckin men at it at again
8
9. A Good Fallout Meme
10. Murder in broad day light
11. Archery 100
12. That cat look
13. Bro misunderstood the assignment
14
15. And I finally I would like to share a concept I'm making for my indie game - a playable menu. So the idea is that you physically play the Main Menu and you can walk around and select what would be the buttons in a normal menu. There is also a short narrative story up ahead. What do you think about the idea? P.S. If it's not too much trouble please support the game with a wishlist, it would mean the world to me: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/

item.title: First Day of School and a New Backpack!
item.description: A beautiful bubble snail cruising the ocean floor. These snails have fragile, small shells that often leave parts of their bodies uncovered. Guided by their prominent headshields, they skillfully burrow through the sand, while a silky layer of mucus ensures their seamless glide through the grains. Sauce: https://www.instagram.com/reel/Cs_6IDbL_oP/

item.title: Some covers that may or may not be as good as the originals but worth a listen.
item.description:
Chris Cornell performing Nothing compares 2 U. Originally performed by Sinead O'Connor, written by Prince https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IuUDRU9-HRk
Miley Cyrus performing Jolene Originally written and performed by Dolly Parton https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wOwblaKmyVw
Mike Masse performing Paranoid Android Originally written and performed by Radiohead https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NVvdjEb0QdA A very cool channel to check out. Many good covers all in some pizza joint.
Ray LaMontagne performing Crazy Originally written and performed by Gnarls Barkley https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BoR1DcFobNE
vkgoeswild performing November Rain Originally written and performed by Axl Rose / Guns n Roses https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FhbeNEtkgDs Another great YT channel worth checking out.
Lisa Hannigan performing Hallelujah Originally written and performed by Leonard Cohen, most famously covered by Jeff Buckley https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aADCdcD1V0k
